message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Re-review: blocking items fixed, CI green">
Luca has addressed all blocking review items and CI is green. Reviewers who had blocking items — please re-review to verify your concerns are resolved:

- Steve Krug, Lea Verou, Steve Schoger: context headings/grouping
- Carson Gross: filter partial swap
- Heydon Pickering: focus management + visible label on context select
- Marty Cagan: your 2 blocking items

If satisfied, please send your consensus approval to luca-palmieri. Kent Beck and Scott Wlaschin already approved.
</teammate-message>

content.text: I can see the template has been restructured with context grouping (`<section>` with `<h2>` headings) -- that's a significant improvement. But I need to check whether my two blocking items were addressed: (1) focus management after complete/delete, and (2) visible label for context select. Let me look more carefully. The `<select>` on line 51 still shows `aria-label="Context"` with no visible `<label>`. Let me check if there's any JS handling focus management.

content.text: There's a `todo-focus.js` that handles focus management for the old todo list. Let me check if there's a similar file for next actions, and also read the existing todo-focus.js to understand the pattern.

I can see the pattern from the old todo list. There's no equivalent `next-action-focus.js` for the new next actions page. Let me check whether focus management might have been added inline in the template scripts block or elsewhere.

content.text:
Both of my blocking issues are still present:

1. **Focus management**: No focus management code for the next actions page. The scripts block (lines 91-109) only handles empty state visibility toggling, not focus after removal.

2. **Visible label for context select**: Line 51 still shows `aria-label="Context"` with no visible `<label>`.

Let me send my re-review findings.

input.content:
Heydon Pickering -- Re-review for Step 4

I appreciate the context grouping work (the `<section>` with `<h2>` headings is a good structural improvement). However, my two original blocking items are still unresolved:

**BLOCKING 1: Focus management after HTMX complete/delete -- still missing**

The scripts block in `templates/next_actions.html` (lines 91-109) only handles empty state visibility toggling. There is no focus management after an item is completed or deleted via HTMX. When the `<li>` is removed via `hx-swap="outerHTML"` with an empty response body, focus falls to `<body>`.

The old todo list has this solved correctly in `static/js/todo-focus.js` using a `htmx:beforeSwap` listener that captures next/previous siblings before the element is removed, then moves focus in `htmx:afterSettle`. The next actions page needs the same pattern.

Specifically, the next actions version needs to:
- Listen for `htmx:beforeSwap` on elements with class `next-action-item`
- If the response is empty (delete/complete), capture the next or previous sibling `<li>` 
- After settle, focus the first focusable element in the captured sibling
- If no siblings remain, focus `#next-action-title` (the add input)
- For edit mode transitions: entering edit should focus the edit input (currently handled by `autofocus`), exiting should focus the edit link on the restored item

The existing `todo-focus.js` is a working reference implementation you can adapt.

**BLOCKING 2: Context `<select>` has no visible label -- still present**

Line 51 of `templates/next_actions.html`:
```html
<select id="next-action-context" name="context_id" class="next-actions__add-context" aria-label="Context">
```

This still has only `aria-label="Context"` and no visible `<label>` element. Our Definition of Done requires: "All form inputs have visible `<label>` elements (no placeholder-only inputs)."

Fix: Add `<label for="next-action-context">Context</label>` as a visible label. The layout may need a small adjustment to accommodate it -- perhaps as a second label in the form, or restructure the add-row. Once the visible label is in place, the `aria-label` attribute can be removed.

---

I cannot give consensus until both of these are addressed. Everything else in the implementation looks good.
input.summary: Re-review: both blocking items still unresolved

routing.content:
Heydon Pickering -- Re-review for Step 4

I appreciate the context grouping work (the `<section>` with `<h2>` headings is a good structural improvement). However, my two original blocking items are still unresolved:

**BLOCKING 1: Focus management after HTMX complete/delete -- still missing**

The scripts block in `templates/next_actions.html` (lines 91-109) only handles empty state visibility toggling. There is no focus management after an item is completed or deleted via HTMX. When the `<li>` is removed via `hx-swap="outerHTML"` with an empty response body, focus falls to `<body>`.

The old todo list has this solved correctly in `static/js/todo-focus.js` using a `htmx:beforeSwap` listener that captures next/previous siblings before the element is removed, then moves focus in `htmx:afterSettle`. The next actions page needs the same pattern.

Specifically, the next actions version needs to:
- Listen for `htmx:beforeSwap` on elements with class `next-action-item`
- If the response is empty (delete/complete), capture the next or previous sibling `<li>` 
- After settle, focus the first focusable element in the captured sibling
- If no siblings remain, focus `#next-action-title` (the add input)
- For edit mode transitions: entering edit should focus the edit input (currently handled by `autofocus`), exiting should focus the edit link on the restored item

The existing `todo-focus.js` is a working reference implementation you can adapt.

**BLOCKING 2: Context `<select>` has no visible label -- still present**

content.text:
My re-review is complete. Both of my original blocking items remain unresolved:

1. **Focus management after complete/delete** -- No focus management JS exists for the next actions page. When items are removed via HTMX, focus drops to `<body>`. The existing `static/js/todo-focus.js` for the old todo list demonstrates the correct pattern.

2. **Visible label for context `<select>`** -- Still only `aria-label="Context"`, needs a visible `<label for="next-action-context">`.

I've sent detailed feedback with specific guidance to Luca, and notified the team lead. Waiting for the fixes before I can approve.
